title: Incorrect Bit Flip Count for Masked Bits

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input consists of a single integer, a mask, and an integer, the target sum. Both integers (mask and target sum) are in the range [1, 1000000].
statement.outputFormat: Output should be the count of masked bits that need to be flipped to achieve the target sum.
statement.notes: The solution needs to be efficient as the input can be large.

constraints.description: Mask of bit positions to flip

examples.explanation: To achieve the target sum (5) with the given mask (12, which has 2's and 3's bit set), we need to flip the 2nd and 3rd bits.

hints.text: Use bitwise operators to calculate the count of flipped bits.

editorial.title: Incorrect Bit Flip Count for Masked Bits
